name: The Rep Playbook
tagline: Everything you wish someone told you before your first rep haul.
tagline_long: The complete field manual for buying reps without getting scammed — sourcing, agents, QC, sizing, customs and legit-checks in one place.
one_liner: The trusted, no-BS field manual that takes a first-time rep buyer from overwhelmed to confident in one read.
aesthetic: Editorial field-manual. Warm paper/cream backgrounds, bold high-contrast serif display, a single confident rust accent, tasteful annotated diagrams and hairline rules. Reads like a well-made handbook written by someone who has actually done every step — the guide, never the salesman. Deliberately the opposite of HaulHQ's dark trader-terminal look so the two brands never blur.

fonts.note: Two-font editorial system. Fraunces (variable soft serif) carries the book-cover / headline character; Inter carries all body, UI, captions and the printable cheat-sheets. Reading long-form in the web reader may optionally use Newsreader for a booky feel, but the shippable default keeps the load to two families. fontKey 'fraunces' is a new pairing for the launchpad fonts.ts (display=Fraunces, text=Inter) — add it there if not present.

display.opsz: Use high optical size (opsz 100+) and SOFT/WONK axes at defaults for the warm editorial cut.
display.usage: Book title / wordmark, hero headlines, chapter numbers and titles, cover spreads, big pull-quotes.
display.tracking: Tighten to -0.01em / -0.02em at large display sizes.

text.usage: Body copy, UI, buttons, labels, captions, FAQ, and all four printable cheat-sheets (kept sans for scannability).

reading_optional.usage: Optional long-form reading serif for the gated web reader body and PDF body if a booky feel is wanted; not loaded by default to keep the site lean.

palette.philosophy: Light-first (paper) is the default everywhere — this is a book, not an app. One rust accent is the only brand color and is reserved for the wordmark mark, primary CTAs, chapter markers and key emphasis — never scattered as decoration. A dark 'ink cover' theme is provided for the hero/cover spread and the OG image where a bold book-jacket feel is wanted. Functional greens/reds/amber exist only for QC and customs semantics (accept / reject / customs-risk) inside diagrams and cheat-sheets.

wcag_aa_pairs.context: light: body ink on paper bg
light: muted text on paper bg
light: muted text on surface panel
light: rust accent as text/link on paper
light: accentDeep small text/link on paper
light CTA: white text on rust fill
light CTA: white text on accentDeep fill
light: accept/QC-pass green text on paper
light: reject/QC-fail red text on paper
dark cover: ink on ink-cover bg
dark cover: muted on ink-cover bg
dark cover: bright rust accent on ink-cover bg

usage_rules: Paper (#FBF6EC) is the default background for the whole site, reader and cheat-sheets. Do not ship a dark app UI — the dark 'ink' theme is only for the cover/hero spread and the OG image.

Rust accent fills only the primary CTA, the logo mark, chapter numbers and key emphasis. Text ON a rust fill is always white (#FFFFFF); use accentDeep (#A3341E) fill when white text needs extra headroom.
For rust as small text or links on paper, prefer accentDeep (#A3341E) — plain accent (#C1432A) passes AA but sits close to the 4.5 floor.

QC diagrams: accept/keep marks use positive green (#2E6B43), reject marks use danger red (#C0392B); customs / de-minimis / seizure risk callouts use warning amber (#B26A00). These are semantic only, never brand decoration.
Rules and card borders are 1px in --border. Editorial flourishes are allowed (thin double rules, drop-caps, chapter numerals) but no drop shadows, no gradients, no neon.

launchpad_css_vars.note: Feed into the launchpad components/theme.ts brandToCssVars() as the Brand object. Light (paper) is the product default; the dark object is for the cover/OG surfaces only.

voice.personality: The trusted guide. Warm, confident, insider-but-welcoming — a friend who has done a hundred hauls sitting you down before your first one. Explains the jargon instead of hiding behind it. Never hypes, never sells hard; earns the cross-sell into HaulHQ and MurmReps by being genuinely useful first.
principles: Teach, don't pitch. Every section leaves the reader more capable, even if they never buy.
Decode the slang the first time you use it (W2C, QC, GL, batch, de minimis) — the reader is a beginner, not stupid.
Be honest about risk and about limits. Customs figures are framed as 2026 estimates, not promises. Trust is the whole product.
Warm and plain-spoken. Short paragraphs, second person, no corporate hedging, no emoji.
Hand off naturally, never pushily: 'run your own numbers in HaulHQ', 'find it on MurmReps' — as helpful next steps, not ads.
do: Before you send a cent, here's how to read a QC photo so you don't accept a bad pair.
W2C just means 'where to cop' — a link to the exact factory listing. Here's how to find a good one.
You will not get every package. Here's how to lower the odds of a seizure and what to do if one lands.
Once you know the steps, run the actual cost of your cart in HaulHQ before you check out.
dont: Unlock the ULTIMATE rep buying secrets they don't want you to know!
The only guide you'll ever need to dominate the rep game.
Get designer looks for 90% off — guaranteed, no risk!
Any emoji-stuffed, hype-driven, guru marketing tone.

logo.concept: An editorial book-jacket lockup: a rust-accented open-manual mark (open book with a bookmark ribbon = a trusted handbook) beside the serif wordmark, with a small tracked 'THE' label above 'Rep Playbook'. Self-contained on a paper panel so it renders on any background, mirroring the HaulHQ approach.
logo.clearspace: Keep clear space equal to the cap-height of the 'R' in 'Rep' on all sides.
variants: full lockup (logo.svg) — paper panel, ink wordmark, rust mark
RP monogram / open-manual mark only (isolate the left glyph) for favicons and avatars
single-color: ink #211C16 on paper, or paper #FBF6EC on the dark ink cover
misuse: Do not recolor the mark/bookmark to anything but the rust accent (#C1432A, or #E86A4E on dark).
Do not set the wordmark in a sans — the serif is the brand's book-cover signal.
Do not add gradients, shadows or outlines; keep it flat and editorial.
Do not stretch, condense or re-space the wordmark.
